item.text:
In [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-hook/demo/rate_limiter.py:15), the minimal fix is already present:

```python
self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
```

The leak happens in the broken version when `allow()` appends every accepted call timestamp to `self.calls` but never removes entries that are older than the rate-limit window. Those stale timestamps can no longer affect the decision, but they still stay referenced by the list, so memory grows with historical traffic.

Minimal fixed `allow()`:

```python
def allow(self) -> bool:
    now = time.time()
    self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
    if len(self.calls) < self.limit:
        self.calls.append(now)
        return True
    return False
```

I also ran `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_rate_limiter`; it passes.
